items.title: Keynote: Architecting Happiness: From Legacy to AI Driven Product Platform
items.speaker: Valtter Rajakannas, Lead Product Owner @OP Financial Group
items.takeaway: How product-platform shifts unlock AI-ready delivery at enterprise scale.
items.abstract: OP Financial Group is Finland's largest financial services group, serving over 4 million customers. In this keynote, Valtter shares how OP is transforming its legacy API platform into an AI-driven product platform to meet evolving customer needs and competitive pressures. He covers the strategic decisions, architectural changes, and organizational shifts required to move from a traditional API gateway to a modern platform that supports AI agents and new consumption patterns. Attendees will learn how to architect their platforms for the future of AI-driven ecosystems while maintaining reliability and security.

items.title: APIOps in 20 Minutes (Orientation)
items.speaker: Marjukka Niinioja, Founding Partner @Osaango
items.takeaway: Get a clear starting point for APIOps—what to implement first and what to ignore to move faster.
items.abstract: APIOps is a practical approach to managing APIs that focuses on continuous improvement and business outcomes. In this orientation session, Marjukka Niinioja breaks down the APIOps and the open APIOps Cycles framework into its essential components, providing a clear roadmap for teams new to APIOps or looking to refocus their efforts. She covers how to prioritize API governance, platform design, and operational practices to deliver value quickly without getting bogged down in unnecessary processes. Attendees will leave with a clear understanding of where to start and how to evolve their APIOps practice over time.

a.title: Your API Strategy Is Not Ready to Compete With AI Investments (Yet)
a.speaker: Claire Barrett, Consultant and API Economist @APIsFirst
a.takeaway: Why most API strategies aren’t able to compete with funding with AI programs and what to do about it.
a.abstract: Many organizations are struggling to align their API strategies with the growing investment in AI. In this session, Claire Barrett explores the gap between API and AI investments and provides actionable insights on how to bridge this divide.

b.title: AI-Native APIs: Designing Platforms for Agentic Systems
b.speaker: Mike Jackson, Founder & CTO @Narranor
b.takeaway: Learn how to design APIs that AI agents can reliably use—not just human developers.
b.abstract: They assume a human reading documentation. Agents don’t “read”—they execute. If your API requires interpretation, it breaks automation. This is the gap: We built APIs for developers. Now we expect machines to use them. At APIOps Helsinki, Mike Jackson is breaking down what *agent-ready APIs* actually require in practice. → Not theory. Design changes.

a.title: SaaS Lives – Value Shifts to the Workspace
a.speaker: Jürgen Buchmann, CEO @TPP Validation
a.takeaway: Understand how value is shifting beyond APIs—and how to reposition your platform accordingly.
a.abstract: Value is moving to: → workflows → agents → user-facing workspaces. APIs are still critical—but increasingly invisible. If your strategy is “more APIs,” you might be optimizing the wrong layer. Jürgen Buchmann will challenge where API value actually lives now.

b.title: No Identity, No Agents: Why IAM Is the Foundation of AI-Driven API Ecosystems
b.speaker: Tomas Bolin, API Customer Success Manager @If Insurance
b.takeaway: Learn how to structure identity and access so your APIs can support AI-driven use cases securely.
b.abstract: Agents don’t just call APIs. They act. That means: - permissions - delegation - traceability. Most IAM setups weren’t designed for that. If identity is wrong, everything built on top is fragile. Tomas Bolin is unpacking what changes when APIs become entry points for autonomous systems.

a.title: Turning API Programs into Revenue
a.speaker: Allan Knabe, CEO @Apiable
a.takeaway: How API platforms move from cost center to measurable business value.
a.abstract: Most API programs will never make a cent. Not because the tech is broken. Because nobody designed them as products. No pricing. No segmentation. No P&L owner. Just a pile of endpoints behind a Swagger page. The shift from cost center → revenue driver isn't a gateway upgrade. It's a product decision your engineering team can't make for you.

b.title: Identity-First APIs: IAM as the Real Platform
b.speaker: Oscar Santolalla, Senior Project Manager @Spinverse
b.takeaway: Why identity becomes the control layer for AI-driven systems.

workshop.title: Design Your APIOps Operating Model (50 min clinic, come when you can, leave when you must)
workshop.speaker: Marjukka Niinioja, Founding Partner @Osaango & APIOps Helsinki Conference Organizer
workshop.takeaway: Define roles, responsibilities, and decision points for your API platform—so execution doesn’t stall.

a.title: Why AI Doesn’t Ship (Yet) — How enterprises move AI into production safely and confidently
a.speaker: Tim Sevenich, Principal Pre-Sales Architect @Axway
a.takeaway: AI isn’t failing in enterprises—it’s stuck at the last mile; this talk shows how to cross it safely, compliantly, and at scale.
a.abstract: Everyone is talking about it, using it to some extend, personally as well as professionally. AI is everywhere these days. But for larger organizations, where it is not yet is: in production. Why is that? And how can you change that with confidence and while mitigating risks? Join this speech to learn how technology might help you leverage the agentic AI revolution while being secure, confident and compliant. Hear our insights into the market from countless conversations with leading customers and learn what might make you accelerate your AI initiatives and move them to production.

b.title: From API Gateways to AI Gateways: The Next Platform Layer
b.speaker: Dan Erez, Chief Architect @Aman
b.takeaway: Understand what needs to change in your gateway layer to support AI agents and new traffic patterns.
b.abstract: Gateways were designed for: → predictable requests → human-driven usage. AI introduces: → bursty behavior → chained calls → non-deterministic patterns. That breaks assumptions. Dan Erez is exploring what an **AI gateway** actually needs to handle.

workshop.title: Clinic CONTINUES: Design Your APIOps Operating Model (come when you can, leave when you must)
workshop.speaker: Marjukka Niinioja, Founding Partner @Osaango & APIOps Helsinki Conference Organizer
workshop.takeaway: Continued: Define roles, responsibilities, and decision points for your API platform—so execution doesn’t stall.

items.title: Panel - Are APIs Still the Platform, or Are AI Agents Replacing Them?
items.speaker: Moderator: Oscar Santolalla, Senior Project Manager @Spinverse, Panelists: Tim Sevenich, Principal Pre-Sales Architect @Axway; Claire Barrett, Consultant and API Economist @APIsFirst; Frank Kilcommins, Head of Enterprise Architecture @Jentic, BGB member @OpenAPI Initiative
items.takeaway: Compare competing platform bets before locking your roadmap.
items.abstract: Two competing views emerging: 1) APIs remain the core abstraction 2) AI agents + identity become the real platform. Both can’t be true long term. At APIOps Helsinki, we’re putting this debate on stage. → Which direction are you betting on?

items.title: Day 2 Opening words
items.speaker: Marjukka Niinioja, Founding Partner @Osaango & APIOps Helsinki Conference Organizer
items.takeaway: Set the stage for a day of hands-on learning and community problem-solving.

items.title: Keynote: From Ecosystem Design to API Strategy
items.speaker: Hanna Sillanpää, Director and Head of Product Line Management @ASSA ABLOY Opening Solutions
items.takeaway: Connect ecosystem decisions directly to platform execution.

a.title: FIDA, Open Finance and the Future of API Ecosystems
a.speaker: Markus Petman, Senior Manager @Fennia
a.takeaway: FIDA turns APIs from integration points into regulated products — and most platforms aren’t ready for that shift.

b.title: Beyond OpenAPI: Workflows That AI Agents Can Actually Execute
b.speaker: Frank Kilcommins, Head of Enterprise Architecture @Jentic, BGB member @OpenAPI Initiative
b.takeaway: Covers Arazzo 1.1 and why workflows matter for agent execution.

workshop.title: Create an MCP Wrapper for Your API (50 min workshop)
workshop.speaker: Jürgen Buchmann, CEO @TPP Validation
workshop.takeaway: Walk away with a working MCP wrapper implementation pattern.

a.title: Boosting API Testing
a.speaker: Katerina Kairo, Senior Consultant
a.takeaway: Test automation patterns and tools to keep up with the speed of API- and AI-driven development.

b.title: Scaling MCP: Avoiding Context Bloat with Gateway & Registry Patterns
b.speaker: Hugo Guerrero, Head of Technical Marketing @Kong & Governance Board Member @AsyncAPI Initiative
b.takeaway: Why MCP implementations hit scaling limits—and how to dynamically route and inject only the tools relevant to each request to maintain performance and reasoning quality.

workshop.title: API Monetization Clinic  (60 min clinic, come when you can, leave when you must)
workshop.speaker: Allan Knabe, CEO @Apiable
workshop.takeaway: Pressure-test your monetization model with practitioners.

a.title: From Dark APIs to Governed Platforms
a.speaker: Thomas Lawrence, Head of Professional Services North EMEA @Kong
a.takeaway: Turn undocumented, drifting APIs into a governed, continuously improving APIOps platform using AI-assisted discovery, GitOps automation, and scalable governance controls.
a.abstract: Most API teams are caught between two uncomfortable realities: APIs in production with no specs, no contracts and no visibility, or platforms where specs exist but governance is already drifting across federated teams. This talk tackles both. Act 1 covers how to discover, retrofit, and productise dark APIs using Kong, GitOps tooling, and AI-generated OpenAPI specs from traffic logs. Act 2 covers what breaks once you get there - standards drift, schema divergence, governance bottlenecks - and how automated linting and AI-assisted gap analysis close the loop. Two acts, two maturity stages, one continuous APIOps journey, with live demos throughout.

b.title: AI Agents and Secure API Access
b.speaker: Merja Kajava, Founder @Aavista
b.takeaway: Learn how to give AI agents access to APIs while maintaining control, security, and auditability.

workshop.title: Clinic CONTINUES: API Monetization Clinic (come when you can, leave when you must)
workshop.speaker: Allan Knabe, CEO @Apiable
workshop.takeaway: Continued: Pressure-test your monetization model with practitioners.

a.title: APIOps Clinic – Strategy, Governance and Platform Design
a.speaker: Coaches: Marjukka Niinioja, Founding Partner @Osaango; Allan Knabe, CEO @Apiable
a.takeaway: Get direct feedback on your governance and operating model.

b.title: AI Lab – APIs, MCP and Agent Integrations
b.speaker: Coaches: Merja Kajava, Founder @Aavista; Jürgen Buchmann, CEO @TPP Validation
b.takeaway: Test agent integrations and identify what breaks in practice.

workshop.title: You May Have OpenAPI, But Is It AI-Ready?
workshop.speaker: Frank Kilcommins, Head of Enterprise Architecture @Jentic
workshop.takeaway: Hands-on scoring and improvement using agent skills combined with open-source frameworks and tooling.

a.title: Guided Unconference – API Strategy and Platform Leadership
a.speaker: Facilitators:  Marjukka Niinioja, Founding Partner @Osaango;Merja Kajava, Founder @Aavista; Allan Knabe, CEO @Apiable
a.takeaway: Surface priority decisions with peers facing the same constraints.

b.title: Guided Unconference – AI Agents and Developer Platforms
b.speaker: Facilitators: Jürgen Buchmann, CEO @TPP Validation; Frank Kilcommins, Head of Enterprise Architecture @Jentic, BGB member @OpenAPI Initiative
b.takeaway: Debate real trade-offs for agent-ready developer platforms.

items.title: APIOps in the Age of AI Agents: What to Prioritize Next
items.speaker: Frank Kilcommins, Head of Enterprise Architecture @Jentic, BGB member @OpenAPI Initiative
items.takeaway: Strategic roadmap for APIOps teams as agents become dominant API consumers.
